operating_state: Amazon reported consolidated net sales of $638 billion in 2024, representing 11% growth driven by AWS acceleration to 19% growth and North America segment expanding 10%. International segment achieved positive operating income reversing prior year losses despite foreign exchange headwinds. Operating income surged 86% to $69 billion as service sales growth outpaced product sales, with AWS and North America generating majority of operating income.
cost_structure: Gross margin improved as cost of sales declined to 51.1% of revenue from 53.0% in the prior year, driven by fulfillment network efficiencies offsetting increased product volumes. Operating expenses grew 6% with technology infrastructure and fulfillment costs increasing from higher sales volumes and AWS capacity investments. Stock-based compensation decreased while depreciation reached $32 billion including accelerated server retirement charges.
material_events: Amazon invested $4 billion in Anthropic convertible notes with additional $2.7 billion commitment through Q4 2025, valued at $13.8 billion using Level 3 inputs. Cash capital expenditures increased to $78 billion from $48 billion in 2023, primarily for technology infrastructure supporting AWS growth. The company extended heavy equipment depreciation from ten to thirteen years while reducing server equipment lives from six to five years, recording $920 million in accelerated depreciation.
macro_exposures: Foreign exchange fluctuations reduced net sales by $2.3 billion with negative comprehensive income impact from currency translation adjustments. The company maintains tax contingencies across multiple jurisdictions including ongoing disputes regarding intangible asset distributions and cloud services taxation. Regulatory scrutiny expanded through formal FTC and state attorney general investigations into competition practices affecting stores, fulfillment network, Prime services, and AWS offerings.
forward_outlook: Management expects Q1 2025 net sales between $151-156 billion representing 5-9% growth with foreign exchange headwinds, and operating income guidance of $14-18 billion. Cash capital expenditures expected to increase in 2025 driven by continued technology infrastructure investments, with AWS performance obligations totaling $177 billion. Extended equipment useful lives will increase 2025 operating income by $900 million while shortened server depreciation reduces it by $700 million.
uncertainty_profile: Multiple antitrust lawsuits pending including FTC case alleging marketplace monopolization seeking structural relief, with patent litigation resulting in $673 million judgment under appeal. Tax contingencies involve interpretations of international tax laws and transfer pricing regulations with resolution timing highly uncertain. Self-insurance liabilities increased to $8.5 billion reflecting elevated claims exposure, while Anthropic investment valuation depends on uncertain liquidity events.
